app.name: CHANGE_ME
app._comment_testDir: Kebab-case folder slug for this target under tests/ (e.g. 'my-app'). Everything for this target lives in tests/<testDir>/ — specs directly inside it, and the page-object helper at tests/<testDir>/helpers/<AppName>Page.ts. Derive it once from app.name (AbvBg -> abv-bg) and keep it stable across runs so tests don't scatter across differently-named folders.
app._comment_allowedURLs: Hard allow-list. The Explore stage refuses to navigate anywhere not listed here — this is the guardrail that stops an agent from wandering onto production or a third-party domain. List only the pages actually explored/tested. If the target is a live third-party site you don't own, keep auth.strategy 'none' and NEVER perform a state-mutating action (login, submit, post).

fresh._comment: used when strategy = fresh-registration. {ts} is replaced with testInfo.workerIndex + Date.now().

existing._comment: used when strategy = existing-credentials. Put real creds in .env, not here — this file is checked into git.
existing._comment_sharedAccount: true = only one session allowed server-side (like Imperia's sqla) -> pipeline runs any suite using this account with --workers=1 and test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' }). Set false if the backend allows concurrent sessions for the same login.
existing._comment_postLoginExtraStep: optional free-text note for a second step after the login button (e.g. a realm/tenant/workspace picker) — the Explore stage documents the exact selector once it finds it.

asyncPattern._comment_type: one of: none | networkResponse. If the app updates the UI via background XHR/fetch calls (Imperia's xajax_loader.php, a GraphQL endpoint, a REST API, ...), set type=networkResponse and give a urlGlob so generated tests wait on the real response instead of a fixed sleep.
